seo.title: Georgia Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Georgia — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license georgia, dental assistant certification georgia, RDA georgia, DANB CDA georgia, dental assistant radiography georgia

board.name: Georgia Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address:
Professional Licensing Boards Division
237 Coliseum Drive
Macon, Georgia 31217-3858

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Georgia.

steps.title: Create an account or log in to the Georgia Online Licensing System.
steps.description: New applicants must register for an account on the Georgia Online Licensing System and create a user profile.

steps.title: Select the appropriate Dental Assistant application.
steps.description: Navigate to the 'Apply for a License' section and select either 'Dental Assistant Qualified to Perform Expanded Duties' or 'Dental Assistant (DA) - Radiology' application within the Georgia Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Complete the online application and upload required documentation.
steps.description: Fill out all sections of the application completely and upload all necessary supporting documents, such as proof of education, BLS certification, and completion of board-approved courses or programs, directly through the Georgia Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Submit payment for the application fee.
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $75 for either Expanded Duties or Radiology certification through the Georgia Online Licensing System using an accepted payment method.

steps.title: Await review and approval by the Georgia Board of Dentistry.
steps.description: The Georgia Board of Dentistry will review your application and supporting documents. Monitor your application status and any messages from the Board via your account in the Georgia Online Licensing System.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Georgia does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Georgia require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Georgia does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Georgia?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Georgia.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Georgia?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Georgia?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Georgia. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Georgia does not license or register general dental assistants. Expanded duty certification available.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Georgia
hero.dek: To apply for a Dental Assistant Expanded Duties or Radiology certificate in Georgia, you must submit your application through the Georgia Online Licensing System.

official_links.registration_steps: First-time applicants must register for an account on the portal by clicking 'Register for an Account', then complete the registration process to start an application.
